programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A girl accidentally kills her fiance just before Dr Sloan arrives at the house for a dinner party - and to make matters worse, the body promptly disappears

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A man holds an antenatal class hostage, and the expectant mothers all start going into labour. Fortunately, Dr Sloan is on hand to resolve matters. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Three talk-show hosts housed in the same studio devise bizarre and outlandish pranks as they vie for ratings, but the tragic result of one joke turns out to be no laughing matter. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team helps Cabe when he receives a distress code from his ex-wife Rebecca. She fears she is being targeted for stumbling upon a money trail, which has links to the assassination of a congressman. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A jet carrying a classified cloaking radar device goes down over Bosnia, and the team helps Navy Seals to find the aircraft so they can recover the technology and locate the pilot's remains. Meanwhile, Walter is forced to bring his sister to meet the team after she has a drunken run-in with the police. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed in what initially seems to be a road-rage incident - until investigations reveal evidence of foul play. Digging deeper, the team finds the dead man belonged to an underground fight club, and sets about discovering whether his involvement was a factor in the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: As Christmas approaches, the team works overtime to solve a robbery - a case made doubly difficult by the fact that the prime suspect is a former petty officer who has supposedly been dead for 17 years. Eventually arrested at a Vietnam memorial, he manages to convince Gibbs of his innocence - but a discovery in the forensics lab suggests he may be guilty after all. Guest starring Peter Coyote

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee is sent to a women's prison to interview a murder suspect, but ends up being captured by the inmates, who have run riot and seized control of a cell block, afraid they will be blamed for killing a guard. Held hostage as a bargaining chip, he tries to identify the real culprit before the warden takes drastic action to resolve the crisis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky is attacked by a knife-wielding woman who accuses him of war crimes, alleging he tortured and killed her brother in Afghanistan in the early 1980s. Gibbs investigates and ascertains what really happened - but has to work with the Afghan embassy to avoid an international incident. Drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the private life of a murdered Navy captain whose actions may have led to his death, while McGee finds his ideal woman on the internet - but it turns out she is too good to be true. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray, Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Scorpion

Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J, Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The agents are puzzled when Hetty assigns them to a missing persons case but gives them precious little information to work with. Callen confronts his diminutive boss and discovers the woman they are looking for is in fact an undercover operative who has been gathering intelligence on a possible nuclear arms dealer. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Deeks goes undercover when a young woman with a penchant for stealing diamonds is targeted by an arms dealer-turned-club owner looking to get back into his original line of work. However, partner Kensi struggles to keep her emotions in check as she runs surveillance on Deeks and his attractive new lady friend

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team resumes its search for the stolen nuclear weapons, with Callen and Kensi heading overseas while Sam and his wife Michelle go back undercover inside Sidorov's arms operation - but shocks lie in store for them along the way. Meanwhile, a familiar face returns to form an unlikely alliance. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J and Christopher Lambert

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Agents G Callen and Sam Hanna investigate after a naval officer is killed during an LAPD shootout, with two other men who died in the gun battle being identified as members of a Mexican drug cartel. Spin-off drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects, an elite NCIS division specialising in undercover assignments. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

Crime drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part story filmed in London and Germany in collaboration with the cast and crew of drama series SOKO: Leipzig. Jack and his team track the kidnap gang to Germany and help arrange a ransom pick-up at Leipzig railway station, but the operation proves to be a set-up leading nowhere. The case takes a violent turn, prompting an unexpected confession from the kidnapped girl's stepfather (Max Herbrechter) and a desperate race to reach the hostages before it is too late

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Nate's day begins in high spirits with a driving assessment supervised by Tony, but events escalate when he fails to keep up with a stolen car, which is later involved in a hit-and-run. The investigations lead to the driver, but also reveal the victim's family has been under intolerable strain, and her brother has been making ends meet by dabbling in motor theft and drug-running

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A teenage runaway is found dead from an overdose near a known drug users' haunt and fingerprints on the syringe suggest she was murdered. Investigations lead Mickey and Jo to a gang trafficking young girls which is part of a national network, and Jo resolves there will be no more victims. Warren Brown guest stars. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jo bonds with a teenager being groomed by the traffickers and Mickey leads a sting to catch the ringleader of the operation. But when the alarm is raised too early, they have insufficient evidence to hold their man, and news then reaches the police that the gang is moving a large group of girls to Glasgow. The race is on to track them down, but Jo's anxiety to save the children clouds her professional judgement

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A rare disease spreads around Poplar Street, and Patsy and Dr Turner try to trace the outbreak to its source. Suspicion over the identity of the first carrier threatens to divide a previously close-knit family. Sister Winifred is forced to confront her prejudices while treating a patient, and Barbara worries about how Trixie will react when she agrees to go to dinner with Tom

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is seconded to a maternity ward, and is impressed by how modern technology is being used to help the mothers - but is left with a moral dilemma after assisting on a traumatic birth. A young man from Poplar is delighted to be accepted into university, but his mother is less than happy. His dreams are jeopardised when his girlfriend gets pregnant, a development that puts pressure on the whole family

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner and Shelagh are at the forefront of the advances in medical technology, and begin a campaign to ensure better respiratory health for their patients. However, Timothy fears their own lifestyle is not good for them and tries to encourage his parents to give up smoking. Violet injures her back and is forced to leave the shop in Fred's hands, a decision that strengthens their relationship but which proves to have other, unforeseen consequences. A new mother's past comes to light, with devastating repercussions for her family. Nurse Crane draws on her own experiences to help her

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Rag Nymph
programme.desc: Feature-length period melodrama set in Victorian times, charting the relationship between feisty Newcastle rag-woman Aggie Winkowski and 10-year-old orphan Millie Forester. Determined to protect Millie from the seamy world of prostitution, Aggie resolves to take the youngster under her wing. Starring Honeysuckle Weeks, Perdita Weeks, Val McLane, Alec Newman, Crispin Bonham-Carter and Patrick Ryecart

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Nurse Crane and Trixie investigate when a mother abandons her children at the clinic, fearing they were being abused or neglected. Her husband admits that she has been behaving strangely lately, and when she is referred to a neurologist by Dr Turner, the truth proves devastating for the whole family. Christopher admits that Alexandra has been unhappy since his divorce, and Violet and Fred organise a beauty contest to raise money for charity. Valerie and Magda both sign up and quickly become close friends - leading to the sharing of a terrible secret

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The shrewd spinster is sent to convalesce in the tropical paradise of Barbados, where she finds life decidedly dull - until a grisly double murder breaks the monotony and presents her with an unforeseen opportunity to do some serious sleuthing. Feature-length mystery, starring Joan Hickson, Donald Pleasence and Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' daring and controversial adaptation of Sarah Waters' novel of lesbian love set in the 1890s. The three-part drama begins as Nan Astley realises she is attracted to other women and embarks on a series of adventures, which take her from her parents' oyster parlour in Whitstable to the glamour of London. Starring Rachael Stirling, Keeley Hawes and Anna Chancellor

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Realising she cannot return to her family life in Whitstable, Nan attempts to earn money by posing as a male prostitute on the streets of London, disguising herself with the aid of stage costumes. It isn't long before she meets a wealthy society widow who offers her a better life - at a perilous price. Rachael Stirling, Keeley Hawes and Anna Chancellor star

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Part three of three. Thrown out by Diana and left destitute by Blake, Nan turns to Florence for help. At first her arrival seems unwelcome, as the idealistic charity worker has settled into a life of domesticity - but the chilly reception soon turns out to be a charade and before long the pair have rekindled their friendship

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealer's visiting American cousin flees the UK with the loot from a raid on Wakering Hall. Feature-length Christmas special from 1993, guest starring John Gielgud and Ken Kercheval

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Peggy argues with Ruth after Vic comes a cropper in Simon's factory, and later comforts Mary Cooper as she tries to come to terms with the death of her husband. Meanwhile, Stephen returns home. Pam Ferris and Sarah Lancashire star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stevie helps Stuart uncover a burglary scam operating from a Santa's grotto run by an ex-con, and a few heads are turned when Father Christmas is brought in for questioning. The trail of evidence leads the team via a loan shark and fake passport dealer to a crime kingpin who is planning to skip the country. He is a man Stevie knows from when she worked undercover, and is convinced he is up to something big

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Greg's emotions run high when he thinks Sahira has been involved in a traffic accident, while Chantelle has to prove herself in the face of demanding patients as the hospital is in the running for Foundation Trust status. Frieda is disappointed about having to work with Luc for the first time, given that she knows about Eddie's departure

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Nadia tries to hide how upset she is by Stitch's reaction to their intimate encounter, before a tryst between the pair leads to tragedy. Maggie pesters Selena to take a pregnancy test, and a patient's mother is forced to face an upsetting truth about her son. Peter O'Brien and Daphne Alexander star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a three-part story. Stuart encourages Stevie to again work undercover as heroin dealer Rob Towler's girlfriend, not realising the chemistry that still exists between them. The case progresses well, especially when Stevie's ex-boss Jim Rutherford turns up to help. Eventually her cover is blown by a member of the gang as the Sun Hill detectives close in for a sting operation, and Stuart is left questioning his colleague's loyalties when she whisks Towler away on her motorbike and disappears without trace. Continues on Monday

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Things are tense between Kathy and Phil. Bianca goes to a clinic where it's confirmed she's pregnant. Michael meets up with his son Matthew - things start off tense

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant, Tiffany, Kathy, Phil, Ricky and Bianca arrive in Paris. Kathy and Phil argue over a letter written to him by Lorna. Kathy gets upset and throws her wedding ring in the Seine

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is delighted when Sarah agrees to marry him. When Elizabeth finds out about the proposal, she realises her threat to disinherit him has backfired - and decides to take a different approach. Elsewhere, Anna encounters complications with her pregnancy, and James and Olivia say goodbye to Harry before they leave Ash Park

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James treats a dog with a confusing complaint, Siegfried tries a herbal remedy on a sick sheep, and Tristan heads off to London for the Coronation. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul's involvement in a political vendetta puts his job on the line. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey and Sharon witness a robbery committed by someone they know, leaving them agonising over whether to turn a blind eye or inform on a mate. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffrey has one too many to drink, and has to be escorted back to his chalet by Gladys. However, Barry and Yvonne witness the spectacle and conclude the two are enjoying an illicit relationship. Comedy, starring Simon Cadell and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth volunteers to help with an OAP day-trip to the seaside, but her plans bring nothing but trouble, landing her in the arms of an amorous Italian pensioner. Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry's promotion at the building society gives him a headache when he has to visit a particularly violent family - and he naively asks Clegg and Truly for advice. Long-running comedy from the pen of Roy Clarke, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Mike Grady

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: A woman dies in the build-up to a Latin dance festival, but the body mysteriously vanishes from the mortuary as Jane and Davey argue over what happened

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: The cop is on a knife-edge as he tries to cover up a death, appease the suspicious Baba and protect Jenny from any further harm. But his problems do not end there - with a killer at large whose actions are ruled by a roll of the dice, he has no way of predicting the next move. Despite a moment of doubt, he puts his plan into action - and is dragged deeper into the criminal world than ever before

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reinvestigates the death of a timeshare magnate, previously thought to have committed suicide, when a reformed call girl claims she was with him the night he died and saw someone force their way into his house as she left. It then emerges the deceased's widow has since fallen on hard times after being swindled by her late husband's business partner - who is connected to a notorious criminal. Guest starring Shane Richie, Naomi Bentley and Claire Rushbrook

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Part two of two. A massive police search leads to the body of a missing five-year-old, the victim of sexual assault and strangulation. All the evidence points to a local drunkard being the guilty party _ but as the case goes to trial it becomes clear that the outcome is by no means assured. Gripping crime drama by Lynda La Plante, starring David Hayman, Kate Buffery, Helen McCrory and Rhys Ifans

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a three-part story. Stuart encourages Stevie to again work undercover as heroin dealer Rob Towler's girlfriend, not realising the chemistry that still exists between them. The case progresses well, especially when Stevie's ex-boss Jim Rutherford turns up to help. Eventually her cover is blown by a member of the gang as the Sun Hill detectives close in for a sting operation, and Stuart is left questioning his colleague's loyalties when she whisks Towler away on her motorbike and disappears without trace. Continues on Monday

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam learns Gene has planted evidence on a notorious armed robber, and decides to let him go. However, the crook then continues his crime spree, critically wounding a cleaner from the police station in the process. The idealistic detective begins to question whether his colleague's approach might be justified - but fears his doubts could affect his chances of returning to the 21st century

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The emotional experience of delivering a baby together helps to reconcile Peggy and Ruth, while Vic learns the reason behind Stephen leaving university. Elsewhere, Mary Cooper causes chaos when she gets behind the wheel of a car. Sarah Lancashire, Pam Ferris, Tony Haygarth and Thomas Craig star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a three-part story. Stuart encourages Stevie to again work undercover as heroin dealer Rob Towler's girlfriend, not realising the chemistry that still exists between them. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira announces she is leaving Holby, but Greg refuses to let her leave without telling her what she means to him. Eddi cannot wait to get away from Luc and AAU, but a power cut forces the pair to work together in a life-threatening situation. Meanwhile, Hanssen has to think on his feet when asked about future plans for the hospital

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: An anxious Selena learns the identity of her baby's father after Maggie persuades her to have a scan, while Charlie worries Stitch is a bad influence on Guppy. The team and the fire brigade are forced to act when the negligence of two drug addicts puts their child's life at risk. A woman rushes to confront her husband who is having an affair, but gets trapped and has to be rescued by Jeff and Dixie

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The evidence against undercover cop Stevie mounts up, suggesting she has thrown away her career for the love of a villain. Stuart finally catches up with the pair, and his suspicions are confirmed when she turns a gun on her colleague and makes her escape. With the violent Tameside Crew wreaking carnage across Sun Hill in pursuit of Towler, Stuart is suspended from duty for being too close to the case. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant checks on Kathy but she's angry with him for not being honest. Kathy starts drinking alone and asks for waiter Emil to deliver some food. Emil makes a pass at Kathy

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy decides to see the sights of Paris on her own. The trip to France also opens old wounds for Tiffany and Grant. Bianca decides to make the most of her last day in Paris

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Anna is relieved when Gino believes she miscarried, and they spend the night together in the cottage before telling their families of their intention to marry. James sees a psychiatrist who agrees to treat his sexuality, Elizabeth worries that Regina is getting closer to discovering the truth about Sarah's past, and Jack is stunned to learn that he is Anna's father. However, a bigger shock is in store for those at Ash Park when a body is found in the river. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: One of Siegfried's old friends lends a hand during an emergency operation, James helps a client avoid arrest, and Helen plans a wedding anniversary dinner. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Lynda Bellingham star

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A straightforward suicide case takes a sinister turn for Paul when he comes to a bizarre conclusion. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant and Michael Melia

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey's house becomes infested with mice, forcing her to shelve plans for a perfume-selling party. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Spike's prospective father-in-law decides it would be a good idea if he were to join the family business. Comedy, starring Paul Shane and Jeffrey Holland

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth prepares for the Mayor's annual fancy-dress ball, only to have her dream of wearing a regal costume shattered. Fortunately, an important new neighbour could provide the chance to claw back some social credibility. Roy Clarke's comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly scares Billy Ingleton out of his wits by mischievously starting a rumour there is a tiger on the loose. The story spreads like wildfire, leading to unexpected consequences for the practical-joking pensioner. Long-running comedy from the pen of Roy Clarke, starring Frank Thornton and guest Norman Wisdom

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A diabetic girl disappears without her insulin, and Lynley discovers she was last seen talking to a couple who had previously been sighted with a youngster later found drowned in a lake. The detective stages a press appeal, hoping to learn more about the mysterious pair and reveal their roles in both cases. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry celebrates his birthday at a comedy club, where a controversial act comes under fire from a group of hecklers. Sandra discovers the most vocal member of the gang is the niece of a political activist who went missing 25 years previously after waging a campaign against the comedian. Agreeing to reinvestigate the disappearance, the squad discovers that the activist and her boyfriend had infiltrated an extreme right-wing group with links to the dangerous criminal who murdered Jack's wife. Guest starring Phil Daniels, Joe Absolom and John Henshaw

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Lynda La Plante's crime drama. DI Pat North investigates the case of a woman abducted in broad daylight, but a sudden murder confession leaves the squad with more questions than answers. Meanwhile, DCS Michael Walker begins to fear for his children's safety at the hands of his ex-wife's new boyfriend. Kate Buffery and David Hayman star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The evidence against undercover cop Stevie mounts up, suggesting she has thrown away her career for the love of a villain. Stuart finally catches up with the pair, and his suspicions are confirmed when she turns a gun on her colleague and makes her escape. With the violent Tameside Crew wreaking carnage across Sun Hill in pursuit of Towler, Stuart is suspended from duty for being too close to the case. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam is called to a murder at a textiles factory, which he is shocked to discover will be his own home in the 21st century. He puts his knowledge of forensics into use to find the killer, but Gene has a simpler approach - the first suspect to speak did it. As battle lines are drawn across CID, it becomes increasingly clear that the Seventies method of detection may in this case be right. John Simm stars

programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of the singles final on day seven of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club. Feliciano Lopez won this title when the tournament was last held in 2019, defeating Gilles Simon in 6-2, 6-7, 7-6. Despite receiving a second-round walkover against the injured Juan Martin del Potro, Lopez very much did things the hard way, with all of his matches going to a deciding set

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Dinosaur
programme.desc: The story of how more than 220 dinosaur bones were found in the Argentinean desert, which were found to have come from a previously undiscovered species, which is the largest land dwelling animal known to have existed. David Attenborough visits the archaeological dig and a laboratory where the remains are being cleaned and analysed with lead scientist Dr Diego Pol and evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod, and meets animators, model-makers, paleontologists and anatomy experts who are working to reconstruct what the 37 metre-long creature would have looked like

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents an archive edition Nature in Art in Gloucestershire, the world's first museum and art gallery dedicated to art inspired by nature. While picking up a few artistic tips, Ellie introduces a selection of stories from the archives that demonstrate how the countryside has been an abiding influence, from early man to modern-day artists braving the extremes to capture a landscape or using wildlife itself to create a picture

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Mark receives word that his father has passed away, and he is given the chance to leave prison for a day to attend the funeral. However, at the last moment, contraband belonging to Daniel is discovered in their cell, and his temporary release is cancelled. He later gets given the chance to take part in a crime and punishment conference, an opportunity Jackson seeks to exploit. Eric is forced to take greater risks to protect his family - but finally has to face the consequences of his actions

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final matches in Group A - Wales v Italy at Stadio Olimpico in Rome and Switzerland v Turkey at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales' triumph against Russia in their concluding group game in 2016 ensured they made it to the knockout stage of a major tournament for the first time since 1958. Today they faced four-time world champions Italy, who have reached at least the quarter-finals of this championship four times out of five this century

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: More highlights from recent series of the motoring show. Featuring another outing for Paddy McGuinness's ice cream van, a tribute to Stirling Moss, the Top Gear Mid Life Crisis Triathlon, and two very different takes on Italian Supercar

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v Wales. A chance to see the final Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. With commentary from Steve Bower and Robbie Savage

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a quirky cottage in Paddock Wood, Kent, a new build in Gnosall, Staffordshire, and a one-bedroom flat in Northampton

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville report on the scammers selling non-existent motorhomes to unsuspecting customers, and the cyber criminals who hijack bookings for holiday rentals

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A distinctive sticker on a laptop finally links a man to a string of burglaries that had terrified a community, and shiny copper hair dye puts a dangerous armed robber behind bars

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Heanor in Derbyshire, where she discovers a rare vase. With experts Thomas Forrester and Catherine Southon

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: As Jimmi tries to make Valerie change her mind, it's Karen's turn to make a rash move. Daniel finds himself in the middle of a complex family situation

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the south Wales countryside to help a couple with £500,000 to spend on relocating from Newport to a village community. While in the region, Nicki visits the only whisky distillery in Wales that uses the mineral-rich water of the Brecon Beacons - to potent effect

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Joanna, Robert and their son Morgan have just moved into their brand-new house in Wrexham. On finding the prospect of sorting out the bare garden quite daunting they call upon Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich to help clear the builders' rubble and provide some ideas. The family has a £6,500 budget to create a garden from scratch that delivers a real 'wow' factor, incorporating dramatic planting, big architectural features and a swanky hot tub area

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including movie posters, Scandinavian furniture and some unusual dolls' heads. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Finland v Belgium (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the final game in Group B for both sides, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium in Russia. Finland, perhaps surprisingly, have an excellent record against the Belgians, and are undefeated against them since 1968, with their two most recent meetings, in 2011 and 2016, both ending in 1-1 draws. They got their campaign under way with a 1-0 victory over Denmark, before losing to Russia by the same scoreline, and will now hope that Roberto Martinez's men will take their foot off the gas, having already secured their place in the next round after winning their opening two matches. Commentary comes from Jonathan Pearce and Martin Keown

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Saskilla examines issues of diversity and inclusion in football over the last few years, talking to Premier League footballers, coaches and managers across the UK, and questioning how much the Football Association has done to fulfil pledges that were made in light of peaceful protests by players in solidarity with the Black Lives Matter movement

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and producer Damian Lewis hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 60th series. Journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events. This new series comes from Hammersmith's Riverside Studios, following the previous run's virtual set-up where the panellists filmed from each of their respective homes throughout lockdown, and is planned to also see the re-introduction of a studio audience

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: RuPaul and judges Michelle Visage and Rhys Nicholson are joined for the grand finale of the series by special guests Olivia Newton-John and her daughter Chloe Lattanzi. By the end of the episode, the first Drag Race winner from Australia or New Zealand will be crowned, and get to add an extra verse to RuPaul's I'm a Winner, Baby

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A full replay from one of today's decisive games, as the group stage of the European Championship nears completion

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts examines the development potential of a mid-terrace house in Suffolk and a semi-detached in Bedfordshire, and Dion Dublin looks over a plot of land in Kent

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The case of a roadside recovery service that left pensioners stranded. Plus, a report on fake travel companies on social media and tips on environmentally friendly camping

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a con woman's fake claims of having cancer were exposed by her distinctive wallpaper, and the hidden compartment in a car that brought a gang defrauding charities to justice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides. Plus, Anita shows off pieces of pottery with a royal connection

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: It's Valerie's last day at the practice and she is planning to go out in style. Thanks to Ruhma, the Mill is alive with the sound of music

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Kamran, Abi, Livi and Nigel vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley heads on a house-hunting mission in rural Devon for a couple who are looking for their first home together, with a budget of £525,000

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team transforms a Reading garden to make it safe for children - despite the fact that no landscaper has been willing to take on the broken slate and rotten decking

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people to the room, with a lazy Susan, Italian jewellery, a cigar case and a ship's wheel on offer to the five professional dealers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben confronts Whitney about spending time with Callum but she tells him to talk to his husband instead. Rocky's interest in the local houses leaves Sonia suspicious, Bobby and Dana take a big step in their relationship and Lola worries about Isaac's erratic behaviour and obsession with Paul

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Fletch's relationship with Evie grows strained as decisions are made about Andrei's future, and he finds himself forced to break her heart to protect his daughter. Ange and Josh try to avoid each other, but must learn to put personal issues aside when a patient's life hangs in the balance. Overwhelmed with guilt, Jeong confesses his mistake with the drugs cupboard to Dom

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Optics expert Richard Biggs works on a home-made telescope, built by the owner's grandfather from bits of scrap during the Second World War. Saddler Suzie Fletcher is brought a leather briefcase once owned by an intrepid traveller, while furniture restorer Will Kirk works his magic on a vintage writing bureau. The final challenge sees Dominic Chinea reviving a rusty child's seesaw that dates from the 1930s and was ahead of its time - not only did it go up and down, but it also spun round. Can Dominic get it spinning again?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team dives into the case of surf-school owner Jake Peters' murder. The victim was shot in his workshop and Humphrey suspects there is more to the incident than meets the eye when he spots the door was locked from the inside with no other obvious means of entry. Suspicion falls on three pupils who have their own agendas, while grieving wife Katie and best friend Karl Slater are harbouring dark secrets. Away from the workplace, Humphrey continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille and decides to take matters into his own hands when faced with competition for her affections. Caribbean-set detective drama, with guest appearances from Hetti Bywater, Claire Goose and Will Mellor

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England and Croatia v Scotland. Action from the final Group D fixtures, which took place at Wembley Stadium and Hampden Park respectively. Gareth Southgate's side were beaten by the Czechs in qualifying for this tournament, a result which ended the Three Lions' 43-game unbeaten run in qualifiers. Scotland had never lost to Croatia in five previous meetings, and manager Steve Clarke will have been hoping for a repeat of their most recent encounter - a 2-0 home win for the Scots in 2013

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to present a countdown of the best midfielders to play in the European Championships

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Romero's newly stable, blissful life is placed in jeopardy by his latest investigation. Meanwhile, Norman considers holding back certain key facts from Dr Edwards. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman is forced to confront his inner demons as his sessions with Dr Edwards progress. Meanwhile, Norma tries to keep her own problems hidden

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England. A chance to see the final Group D fixture for both sides, which took place at Wembley Stadium. With commentary from Steve Wilson and Danny Murphy

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: The Gardeners' World presenter's year-long diary about the life cycle of her garden at Glebe Cottage in Devon. In summer, Carol deadheads the roses and stakes plants to help keep the plot looking its best and savours the full blooms of geraniums and lilies. She creates colourful borders using cannas, ginger and dahlias, before visiting her local beach, Braunton Burrows, to seek out sea holly, which she hopes to grow herself

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Gardeners in Goring-on-Thames, Oxfordshire, aim to transform the train station, brighten up the pathway to the high street and fill the village with hanging baskets

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As midsummer approaches, it's the perfect time for Monty Don to place succulents outside at Longmeadow, as well as planting out dahlias and tender vegetables. Carol Klein visits Trebah Garden in Cornwall, where she finds plants which can add early summer pazazz to borders. Nick Bailey is at RHS Wisley to look at a variety of shrubs that can be used as alternatives to traditional box hedging

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani head to Co Down to catch up with the dairy farming Lilburn family. Elsewhere, Adam Henson discovers how science is helping to save rare breeds, Charlotte Smith investigates a shadow pandemic of rural domestic violence, and John Craven launches the 30th Countryfile Photographic Competition

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond is in Sutherland to see why Forestry & Land Scotland (formerly the Forestry Commission) is pulling up trees and restoring peatland. Euan McIllwraith meets the author of the Scots Dictionary of Nature, the sparks are flying as JJ Chalmers tries a bit of blacksmithing, and Anne Lundon puts on her bee suit to visit the hives of a 'bee whisperer'

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson is back in her Inverleith Park allotment, where she is setting up a wormery and looking forward to a pretty and productive harvest. Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray show how to prune and tame overgrown evergreens, and the Beechgrowers return with further updates in their gardening video diaries from the length and breadth of Scotland

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Double Dynamite (1951)
programme.desc: A penniless bank teller comes to the aid of a bookie, who returns the favour by placing a bet on his behalf. The good Samaritan ends up a wealthy man as a result, but when his workplace is robbed, he has a hard time proving to the police where his newfound riches came from. Comedy, starring Frank Sinatra, Jane Russell and Groucho Marx

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones celebrates Father's Day with a look at inspirational dads, with award-winning rap artist and author Guvna B chatting to Radzi Chinyanganya

programme.title: How the West Was Won (1962)
programme.desc: Three generations of a pioneering family experience many ordeals and adventures in their endeavour to build a new life for themselves on the American frontier in the 19th century. Over the years, the family experiences the key events of the nation's history, including the gold rush, the Civil War, and the building of the railroads. Epic Western, starring James Stewart, John Wayne, Gregory Peck, Henry Fonda, Debbie Reynolds, Lee J Cobb and Richard Widmark

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Will Axon assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, including a Busby hat and a Worcester coffee set. Presenter Paul Martin explores the Battle of Edgehill

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The photographers face three final assignments, including a wildcard challenge where they are given free rein to try and get one last extraordinary shot for their portfolios. They are also in Glencoe for a dramatic landscape challenge and must curate and mount what they hope will be a winning exhibition for the judges. Choosing a maximum of 15 images from the hundreds they've taken across the course of the series is a huge task in itself

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Lynn and Sandra in the Highlands get a surprise when they harvest their first batch of honey. The Rodgers family gather to give bull Mario a good send-off, while Raymond and Johnny discover their sheep are under threat from flystrike, and the only way to protect them is to put the entire flock of 1,600 through the sheepdip

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: Ade explores central Africa, travelling through Gabon, the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. He investigates plans to develop a sustainable version of palm oil farming, meets a champion boxer helping to rehabilitate child soldiers, and talks to one of Africa's most outspoken political campaigners. He also reports on how conflict between militia groups influenced by foreign powers lies at the heart of the problems that have made the Democratic Republic of Congo arguably the most chaotic and corrupt country in Africa, as well as discovering how the conflict has impacted on the country's few remaining gorillas

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters look back on trips across the continent, with Steve Backshall reflecting on being dropped into the nest of a wild saltwater crocodile to help scientists in the Northern Territory to assess the species' health. Gordon Buchanan reveals how faced his own fear of sharks when he visited Owarigi in the Solomon Islands, and Liz Bonnin looks back on witnessing the hatching of baby green sea turtles on Heron Island on the Great Barrier Reef,

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: Brian explores the enigma of time, a phenomenon that is taken for granted but which, as far as physicists are concerned, is one of the biggest mysteries in the universe. He recalls highlights from his TV series that touch upon different sides of this physics conundrum, from breaking the sound barrier in a Eurofighter in order to see two sunsets in one day, to witnessing one of the oldest animal life cycles on Earth on a remote beach in Costa Rica

programme.title: Arctic with Bruce Parry
programme.desc: The explorer travels to the far north of Canada, where he joins members of the Gwich'in tribe as they prepare to head out on their annual spring caribou hunt - and discovers why the tradition has come under threat from oil exploration. He then learns more about the industry's impact on the native way of life when he visits the Alberta tar sands, home to the world's second-largest oil reserves

programme.title: Andy Murray: King of Queen's
programme.desc: A look at the tennis star's success in the Wimbledon warm-up event at Queen's Club in London, featuring footage of some of memorable matches from his five singles title wins. Murray has won a record five singles titles on the immaculate grass courts at Queen's. It was the scene of his first win on the ATP Tour back in 2005, and both of his Wimbledon titles were prefaced with victory at the west London venue, which is one of the most prestigious events in men's tennis and one of the highlights of the British sporting summer. The programme features some of his biggest triumphs, including his emotional victory with Feliciano Lopez in the men's doubles there last year, just a few months after major hip surgery

programme.title: Amundsen (2019)
programme.desc: Film drama telling the life story of explorer Roald Amundsen. Inspired by a gift from his father when he was a child, Roald became fascinated by the polar wildernesses. He led the first expedition to the South Pole in 1911 and the first expedition proven to have reached the North Pole, in a dirigible, in 1926. His achievements, however, came at a cost to both his family and his colleagues. Starring Pal Sverre Hagen and Katherine Waterston. In Norwegian and English

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian finds it hard to keep his cool when Fletch blames him for Evie's disappearance. Sacha and Dom prepare to be interviewed for the clinical lead job. Will Sacha's experience win against Dom's ruthless ambition? Josh is determined to crack a mystery case, hoping to impress Ange and rekindle their relationship

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes a tour across the UK looking at some of the most memorable glass items bought by competing teams, before seeing how they fared at auction. He also tries his hand at glass cutting and shares his tips when it comes to buying and selling glass. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp blows some glass and Roo Irvine discusses her passion for glass with Charlie Ross

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal-worker Dominic Chinea has a treat in store when he works on a rare bicycle sidecar that has not been used in decades, while furniture restorer Will Kirk needs all his skills when he is presented with a box of bits that used to be a Victorian sewing table, crushed to pieces in an accident. Can he piece this fragile 3D jigsaw back together? Percussion expert Pete Woods takes receipt of a military drum that saw frontline action in both world wars and horologist Steve Fletcher tries to get an art deco clock chiming again

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Talking Tennis
programme.desc: Part one of two. Alistair McGowan celebrates the annual Wimbledon Championships, beginning by highlighting some of the greatest tennis rivalries to emerge on the lawns of SW19

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of day one from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club, featuring first-round matches. This tournament was not held in 2020 due to the coronavirus pandemic, meaning the players involved will be more eager than ever to gain valuable grass-court experience before Wimbledon gets under way as they attempt to win the title claimed by the Czech Republic's Karolina Pliskova two years ago

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Will Axon and Mark Stacey visit the Oxford Union to admire another selection of the public's personal treasures, including a First World War bayonet. Plus, presenter Paul Martin charts the birth of rock 'n' roll through the work of music photographer Harry Hammond

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through Germany, investigating the beginning of Bauhaus design in Weimar and visiting the movement's first building, before taking a ride in a high-performance Porsche to the manufacturer's Stuttgart headquarters

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Russia v Denmark (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the final Group B game for both countries, which takes place at Parken in Copenhagen. Somewhat surprisingly, this is only the second-ever meeting between these nations, and the first since February 2012. In a friendly on this same ground, it was the Russians who emerged victorious, with Roman Shirokov and Andrey Arshavin finding the net for Dick Advocaat's men. Russia lost 3-0 to Belgium in their opener, but bounced back with a 1-0 win over Finland, while the Danes have lost both their matches so far by one-goal margins in a campaign overshadowed by talisman Christian Eriksen's cardiac arrest in their opener, but they could still potentially progress to the knockout stage with a victory this evening. Commentary comes from Guy Mowbray and Danny Murphy

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: David Beckham: For the Love of the Game
programme.desc: Cameras follow David Beckham as he attempts to play a football match on all seven continents and get back in time for his own UNICEF fundraising match at Old Trafford. On the journey, he discovers what football means to the many different people he meets and plays with, as well as some of the universal truths about the game itself, including its ability to inspire and unite people

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani head to Co Down to catch up with the dairy farming Lilburn family. Elsewhere, Adam Henson discovers how science is helping to save rare breeds, Charlotte Smith investigates a shadow pandemic of rural domestic violence, and John Craven launches the 30th Countryfile Photographic Competition

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 lockdown beings to ease, the Emergency Department at University Hospital, Coventry experiences an increase in patients presenting mental health concerns. Faced with cases of self-harm and suicide, NHS staff must deal with a dwindling number of mental health beds when trying to give patients the care they need

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Heanor in Derbyshire, where she discovers a rare vase. With experts Thomas Forrester and Catherine Southon

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don turns his attention to containers, planting them up for drama and summer colour in the jewel garden and showing us how to re-pot citrus plants. In Hampshire, Joe Swift meets the designer of an award-winning contemporary garden to explore the techniques and the plants she has used to seamlessly integrate both house and garden

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Warrington Wolves, Huddersfield Giants v Salford Red Devils and Catalans Dragons v Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of day two from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club, featuring second-round matches. Karolina Pliskova was victorious when this competition was last stage in 2019 and she got her tournament under way at this stage with a routine 6-3, 6-3 victory over Margarita Gasparyan. British number one Johanna Konta also booked her place in the third round when she prevailed 6-4, 7-6 against Maria Sakkari

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team values antiques at Guildford Cathedral, where James Lewis discovers a brass figure that could be six centuries old, and Catherine Southon examines a pocket watch with a difference. Paul Martin takes time out to look at the world's first motor-racing track, and goes for a spin in a 1930s sports car

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journey brings him to the Italian island of Sicily. In the capital city of Palermo, he takes in the art and architecture of the Futurists and feasts on spaghetti and sardines in the city's Ballaro market. Then, he makes his way through the ancient town of Agrigento and the port of Siracus

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: John Cooper Clarke and Phill Jupitus embark on the cross-country treasure-hunting challenge in East Anglia, as well as taking a look at the Norwich School of 19th-century artists

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon presents a home makeover show with a difference, as cutting-edge virtual reality and visual effects enable people to see what the future of their home could look like, before it is built in reality. In the first edition, two architects compete to remodel a home in Scotland for 34-year-old Andy and his 32-year-old partner Esther, whose three-bedroom house in Stirling is now too cramped for the pair and their three small children. But which of the two radical new designs will then be built in real life?

programme.title: The Countess and the Russian Billionaire
programme.desc: An inside look at the secret world of Sergei Pugachev and his British partner Countess Alexandra Tolstoy. A hugely rich and powerful couple, with three beautiful children, homes across the world, and unimaginable wealth, the couple appeared to have it all, but their fairy tale life is about to come crashing down around them

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig invites Aisling Bea, Corey Taylor and Ross Noble and Alan Davies to answer questions on subjects associated with nosey and noisy. Since the questions are impossible to answer, points are awarded for being interesting rather than correct

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: As news of the Covid-19 virus broke around the world, teams of scientists jumped into action to create a vaccine against a virus that no one had heard of before. This programme looks at the extraordinary inside story of the unprecedented quest to develop vaccines to fight Covid-19, with unique access to five teams of scientists around the globe. Narrated by Lesley Sharp

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part one of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first Northern Ireland parliament. Fearful for their lives, they had come to a city scarred by bitter sectarian violence. One hundred years on, this is the story of the dramatic events that led to the partition of Ireland - one that continues to reverberate to the present day

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part two of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first parliament of Northern Ireland. The King's visit to Belfast was the culmination of three centuries of history. This concluding film examines how political tensions and outbreaks of sectarian violence threatened to destabilise the new Northern Irish state

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works with one of the UK's biggest water companies. He traces the flow of this precious resource from the reservoir to homes and businesses, before finding out what happens with wastewater at the other end of the system

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Featuring an island paradise for puffins in Scotland and golden hares in Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, Chris Packham checks in on the live nests, and Michaela Strachan reports on the plight of the Norfolk plovers

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Josie d'Arby presents the first in a series of performances by BBC Orchestras providing an in-depth exploration of famous pieces of classical music. At Glasgow City Halls, the BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra play Gershwin's Rhapsody in Blue, with piano soloist Marc André Hamelin and conductor Thomas Dausgaard. Featuring contributions from composer Julian Joseph and biographer David Benedict

programme.title: Maps: Power, Plunder and Possession
programme.desc: Professor Jerry Brotton explains the creation and importance of maps, discovering the latest technology being used to improve the cartographer's art and revolutionise mankind's knowledge of the world. On a visit to the oldest known map, etched into a hillside 3,000 years ago, he considers how different cultures have approached map-making over millennia, often as a tool for expansionism and political control

programme.title: World War Two: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: An examination of the USSR's alliances during World War Two. Laurence Rees hears evidence of Stalin's secret allegiances in the late 1930s, which demonstrate the importance of the Soviet switch to Britain and America's side after its betrayal by the Nazis at the height of war in 1941. He discovers secrets hidden by Russia for more than 50 years and hears from previously silent KGB officers

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: The broadcaster and journalist interviews the TV and film writer about his life and career, covering such topics as his memories of working-class Liverpool, how his struggles with speech drove his love of words and the impact of Catholicism on him. The pair also reflect on his writing achievements, from Brookside and Cracker through to controversial 1994 film Priest and the BBC drama Accused

programme.title: Fathers and Songs: Music for Father's Day
programme.desc: A selection of songs that celebrate fatherhood and probe the emotional complexities of a man's relationship with his son or daughter. Featured artists of past and present include Kid Creole and the Coconuts, Cat Stevens, Emmylou Harris, Paul Simon, Madonna, Peter Gabriel, Mike and the Mechanics, Suggs and the Blockheads, Neil Young, James Brown, Ozzy and Kelly Osbourne and Pigbag

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Film-maker Morgan Matthews' documentary charts 10 years in the life of his father Geoff, following him through a financial crisis and the onset of emphysema and cancer

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Josie d'Arby presents the first in a series of performances by BBC Orchestras providing an in-depth exploration of famous pieces of classical music. programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to create a beautiful summer getaway with rugged brown peaks, clear blue waters and a cosy little cabin

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Classic travelogue featuring singer Jane McDonald. Go behind the scenes of luxury cruise ship Galaxy as she sails around the Caribbean. Originally broadcast in 1998

programme.title: Viceroy's House (2017)
programme.desc: The final Viceroy of India, Lord Mountbatten, oversees the transition of British India to independence, but meets with conflict as opposing sides clash in the face of huge change. Drama, starring Hugh Bonneville, Gillian Anderson and Manish Dayal

programme.title: India's Partition: The Forgotten Story
programme.desc: Film director Gurinder Chadha explores the events surrounding the Partition of India in 1947, which saw the creation of the new nation of Pakistan and led to violence, disruption and death. Gurinder crosses India, meeting people whose lives were torn apart by the division and talking to historians to find out the motivations behind the split

programme.title: The Stolen Maharajah: Britain's Indian Royal
programme.desc: The story of Duleep Singh, the last Maharajah of the Punjab, who was captured by the British army as a boy in the 1840s and raised in England as an aristocrat. The young man rose through British society to become a friend of Queen Victoria - but when he realised what had been done to him, he sought to reclaim his stolen Sikh heritage and became a rebel against imperial rule in India

programme.title: Churchill and the Movie Mogul
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the friendship and collaboration between Winston Churchill and the film producer Alexander Korda. In 1934, Korda signed Churchill up as a screenwriter and historical advisor, and Churchill provided script notes for Korda's productions and penned a screenplay. When the Second World War broke out, Korda was sent on a mission to Hollywood to help bring America into the war, with positive results

programme.desc: Documentary following veteran civil rights lawyer-turned-district attorney Larry Krasner as he adopts a radical approach to criminal justice in Philadelphia

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon charts the history of American art on a coast-to-coast journey, beginning in the east, where he discusses how the country's rise to power was underpinned by the destruction of Native American cultural heritage. He examines portraits of Puritan settlers in Massachusetts, tells the story of a famous painting of Quaker settler William Penn, reads one of the world's most expensive books in Philadelphia and explore the wilderness landscapes that inspired artist Thomas Cole

programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Theo, Dionne, Francis, Oliver, Minnie and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel’s got some socks, a bib and a wooly hat inside his bag. All the parents and babies have different dressing-themed toys and objects too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide safe, baby-appropriate clothes or dressing-up toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to explore with their baby. Some viewers show how they’ve been getting dressed at home too. The fun song We All Like Getting Dressed features, as does a story about Baby Bear dressing up in different outfits.
