programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po want to go outside, but it's raining

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Niger and Baby Bear meet babies Isobel, Idris, Lincoln, Amber, Amelia, Jason and Willow.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: Wingston thinks it's monster-mazing to have a wobbly tooth until he learns that, very soon, he'll get a visit from the tooth fairy

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: B.O.T. drops into Spacey Glowy World and down a crater into darkness. A glowing trail of the trumpeting Squiggling Tooter can be seen through the dark tunnels which leads to a cave full of dancing, honking, partying Tooters!

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day knows young animals make great climbers. In North America black bear cubs must learn the right tree. A slippery river bank is a challenge for giant river otters in Brazil. In Israel special goats called ibex face a clifftop walk

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on a walk to play Billy Goat's Gruff!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a shepherd catching balls and a costume tower. Book Boy makes a computer

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid is enlisted to make a wedding cake for an unhappy bride, and makes his way to a bakery to find an assistant

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Bobble refuses to climb a mountain, until Flicky Flack helps him forget how high it is

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: GrimHQ crashes into the Baobab trees

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy travels 100 million years back into prehistoric times to meet spinosaurus, a massive meat-eating dinosaur even bigger than a T-Rex. On his adventure he rides a speedy ouranosaurus and takes a dip in a prehistoric river

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle learns that the summer holidays will soon be over

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Miss Bowline-Hitch's sundial needs the sun to shine on it, and Jack recalls a treasure hunt that needed daylight to solve

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: A trip to the funfair scares Tig, but Woolly helps her overcome her fears

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘bread’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. This episode is all about bread – brown bread, flat bread, long bread, toasted bread, butterfly bread and rainbow bread. Dee and a friend roll bread in a ball, and we see a ‘wear it on your head’ bread. The journey is repeated with some surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Introducing the letter M as the ever-munching M and friends make a gingerbread man and chase after him on a flying mat - using a handy map to keep them hot on his trail

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The big squares set out to land a square on the moon using square power alone. Learn about big square numbers with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Pug makes a kite, but he doesn’t know how to fly it. He and Dog go to China and meet two squabbling Pandas looking for bamboo shoots. Pug and his kite get blown up in the air, and he lands in a bamboo forest. The Pandas then help him with his kite.

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy goes to Okido and finds out electricity can lead to music, lights and disco dancing

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour yellow, from cheese to chicks, and we explore yellow in spring time. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour yellow.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Using a sign language called Makaton, Something Special helps its audience learn how to communicate as well as develop their language in a fun and exciting way. Justin meets some musical friends and they put on a concert. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is the judge of Tumbleton's Got Talent! starring Grandad Tumble, Lord Tumble and Aunt Polly

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po drive to the Hidey Hup in the Tubby Car

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A three-year-old bunny meets friends and experiences emotions as he engages with the world around him

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The sheep learns to play the xylophone, but loses one of the chimes

programme.title: Footy Pups
programme.desc: Rachel Yankey and the team practise pacing themselves by jogging, running and sprinting. John Motson narrates

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, CBeebies’ very own motorbike rider, Grace, shows off three amazing machines that all belong in the swamp. First Grace goes for a ride in an airboat and shows off an unusual corkscrew-propelled machine, and then she goes for a drive in a swamp buggy.

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain knits some new uniforms for the crew

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: A very sleepy Tommy Brock is hoisted up into the Squirrel Camp, so Peter and friends try to work out how to get the snoozing badger back down to earth before he wakes up

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Bishop Rose introduces us to her majestic Mitre that she wears when she leads services in the Church of England.

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: The presenter goes back in time to search for a dung beetle for the museum, but is distracted by a baby diplodocus taking its first steps

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is looking at useful lights

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Mum and Dad create their own Magic Claw machine at home, but can Bluey win the big prize?

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch takes all of the colourful powder from the Festival of Colour in Dwarka, India.

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Mummy asks the twins to sort out their old toys. She wants them to give away the ones they do not use any more before they move house

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is staying at Angela's kennels while the Brooklyn-Bells are on holiday.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran fix up Gran Gran's old bike and cycle through the park together.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah is hoping for a nice quiet birthday, but does not get her wish once her friends arrive

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Stories for pre-school children, featuring comical toy characters living in a toy house

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major invents something new, and Tiny and Small give him an idea

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Tombliboo Eee accidentally knocks over Ooo's tower of bricks, causing him to play sad music on the piano

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Children's programme.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher presents topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double bill from the Newark antiques fair, with two sets of reds and blues being helped by experts Ben Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of the singles final on day seven of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club. Feliciano Lopez won this title when the tournament was last held in 2019, defeating Gilles Simon in 6-2, 6-7, 7-6. Despite receiving a second-round walkover against the injured Juan Martin del Potro, Lopez very much did things the hard way, with all of his matches going to a deciding set

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a pair of ex-pats who are raring to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Dinosaur
programme.desc: The story of how more than 220 dinosaur bones were found in the Argentinean desert, which were found to have come from a previously undiscovered species, which is the largest land dwelling animal known to have existed. David Attenborough visits the archaeological dig and a laboratory where the remains are being cleaned and analysed with lead scientist Dr Diego Pol and evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod, and meets animators, model-makers, paleontologists and anatomy experts who are working to reconstruct what the 37 metre-long creature would have looked like

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents an archive edition Nature in Art in Gloucestershire, the world's first museum and art gallery dedicated to art inspired by nature. While picking up a few artistic tips, Ellie introduces a selection of stories from the archives that demonstrate how the countryside has been an abiding influence, from early man to modern-day artists braving the extremes to capture a landscape or using wildlife itself to create a picture

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Mark receives word that his father has passed away, and he is given the chance to leave prison for a day to attend the funeral. However, at the last moment, contraband belonging to Daniel is discovered in their cell, and his temporary release is cancelled. He later gets given the chance to take part in a crime and punishment conference, an opportunity Jackson seeks to exploit. Eric is forced to take greater risks to protect his family - but finally has to face the consequences of his actions

programme.title: Elis James' Feast of Football
programme.desc: Elis James is joined by Danny Gabbidon and Natasha Harding to look back at Wales' final group A match against Italy

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final matches in Group A - Wales v Italy at Stadio Olimpico in Rome and Switzerland v Turkey at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales' triumph against Russia in their concluding group game in 2016 ensured they made it to the knockout stage of a major tournament for the first time since 1958. Today they faced four-time world champions Italy, who have reached at least the quarter-finals of this championship four times out of five this century

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following two men preparing to compete in the World Stone Skimming Championships, held on the tiny west coast island of Easdale in Scotland. Dougie is eight-time World Stone Skimming champion and native Scot from Blairgowrie, and although he is confident, newcomer Alex threatens his legendary status. The programme charts the determination and dedication of the competitors, and reveals what it truly takes to come out on top in this unique contest

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v Wales. A chance to see the final Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. With commentary from Steve Bower and Robbie Savage

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a quirky cottage in Paddock Wood, Kent, a new build in Gnosall, Staffordshire, and a one-bedroom flat in Northampton

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville report on the scammers selling non-existent motorhomes to unsuspecting customers, and the cyber criminals who hijack bookings for holiday rentals

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A distinctive sticker on a laptop finally links a man to a string of burglaries that had terrified a community, and shiny copper hair dye puts a dangerous armed robber behind bars

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Heanor in Derbyshire, where she discovers a rare vase. With experts Thomas Forrester and Catherine Southon

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: As Jimmi tries to make Valerie change her mind, it's Karen's turn to make a rash move. Daniel finds himself in the middle of a complex family situation

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the south Wales countryside to help a couple with £500,000 to spend on relocating from Newport to a village community. While in the region, Nicki visits the only whisky distillery in Wales that uses the mineral-rich water of the Brecon Beacons - to potent effect

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Joanna, Robert and their son Morgan have just moved into their brand-new house in Wrexham. On finding the prospect of sorting out the bare garden quite daunting they call upon Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich to help clear the builders' rubble and provide some ideas. The family has a £6,500 budget to create a garden from scratch that delivers a real 'wow' factor, incorporating dramatic planting, big architectural features and a swanky hot tub area

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including movie posters, Scandinavian furniture and some unusual dolls' heads. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Finland v Belgium (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the final game in Group B for both sides, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium in Russia. Finland, perhaps surprisingly, have an excellent record against the Belgians, and are undefeated against them since 1968, with their two most recent meetings, in 2011 and 2016, both ending in 1-1 draws. They got their campaign under way with a 1-0 victory over Denmark, before losing to Russia by the same scoreline, and will now hope that Roberto Martinez's men will take their foot off the gas, having already secured their place in the next round after winning their opening two matches. Commentary comes from Jonathan Pearce and Martin Keown

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Saskilla examines issues of diversity and inclusion in football over the last few years, talking to Premier League footballers, coaches and managers across the UK, and questioning how much the Football Association has done to fulfil pledges that were made in light of peaceful protests by players in solidarity with the Black Lives Matter movement

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and producer Damian Lewis hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 60th series. Journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events. This new series comes from Hammersmith's Riverside Studios, following the previous run's virtual set-up where the panellists filmed from each of their respective homes throughout lockdown, and is planned to also see the re-introduction of a studio audience

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: RuPaul and judges Michelle Visage and Rhys Nicholson are joined for the grand finale of the series by special guests Olivia Newton-John and her daughter Chloe Lattanzi. By the end of the episode, the first Drag Race winner from Australia or New Zealand will be crowned, and get to add an extra verse to RuPaul's I'm a Winner, Baby

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A full replay from one of today's decisive games, as the group stage of the European Championship nears completion

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts examines the development potential of a mid-terrace house in Suffolk and a semi-detached in Bedfordshire, and Dion Dublin looks over a plot of land in Kent

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The case of a roadside recovery service that left pensioners stranded. Plus, a report on fake travel companies on social media and tips on environmentally friendly camping

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a con woman's fake claims of having cancer were exposed by her distinctive wallpaper, and the hidden compartment in a car that brought a gang defrauding charities to justice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides. Plus, Anita shows off pieces of pottery with a royal connection

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: It's Valerie's last day at the practice and she is planning to go out in style. Thanks to Ruhma, the Mill is alive with the sound of music

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Kamran, Abi, Livi and Nigel vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley heads on a house-hunting mission in rural Devon for a couple who are looking for their first home together, with a budget of £525,000

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team transforms a Reading garden to make it safe for children - despite the fact that no landscaper has been willing to take on the broken slate and rotten decking

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people to the room, with a lazy Susan, Italian jewellery, a cigar case and a ship's wheel on offer to the five professional dealers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben confronts Whitney about spending time with Callum but she tells him to talk to his husband instead. Rocky's interest in the local houses leaves Sonia suspicious, Bobby and Dana take a big step in their relationship and Lola worries about Isaac's erratic behaviour and obsession with Paul

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Fletch's relationship with Evie grows strained as decisions are made about Andrei's future, and he finds himself forced to break her heart to protect his daughter. Ange and Josh try to avoid each other, but must learn to put personal issues aside when a patient's life hangs in the balance. Overwhelmed with guilt, Jeong confesses his mistake with the drugs cupboard to Dom

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Optics expert Richard Biggs works on a home-made telescope, built by the owner's grandfather from bits of scrap during the Second World War. Saddler Suzie Fletcher is brought a leather briefcase once owned by an intrepid traveller, while furniture restorer Will Kirk works his magic on a vintage writing bureau. The final challenge sees Dominic Chinea reviving a rusty child's seesaw that dates from the 1930s and was ahead of its time - not only did it go up and down, but it also spun round. Can Dominic get it spinning again?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team dives into the case of surf-school owner Jake Peters' murder. The victim was shot in his workshop and Humphrey suspects there is more to the incident than meets the eye when he spots the door was locked from the inside with no other obvious means of entry. Suspicion falls on three pupils who have their own agendas, while grieving wife Katie and best friend Karl Slater are harbouring dark secrets. Away from the workplace, Humphrey continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille and decides to take matters into his own hands when faced with competition for her affections. Caribbean-set detective drama, with guest appearances from Hetti Bywater, Claire Goose and Will Mellor

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England and Croatia v Scotland. Action from the final Group D fixtures, which took place at Wembley Stadium and Hampden Park respectively. Gareth Southgate's side were beaten by the Czechs in qualifying for this tournament, a result which ended the Three Lions' 43-game unbeaten run in qualifiers. Scotland had never lost to Croatia in five previous meetings, and manager Steve Clarke will have been hoping for a repeat of their most recent encounter - a 2-0 home win for the Scots in 2013

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to present a countdown of the best midfielders to play in the European Championships

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Romero's newly stable, blissful life is placed in jeopardy by his latest investigation. Meanwhile, Norman considers holding back certain key facts from Dr Edwards. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman is forced to confront his inner demons as his sessions with Dr Edwards progress. Meanwhile, Norma tries to keep her own problems hidden

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England. A chance to see the final Group D fixture for both sides, which took place at Wembley Stadium. With commentary from Steve Wilson and Danny Murphy

programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the south Wales countryside to help a couple with £500,000 to spend on relocating from Newport to a village community. While in the region, Nicki visits the only whisky distillery in Wales that uses the mineral-rich water of the Brecon Beacons - to potent effect

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Joanna, Robert and their son Morgan have just moved into their brand-new house in Wrexham. On finding the prospect of sorting out the bare garden quite daunting they call upon Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich to help clear the builders' rubble and provide some ideas. The family has a £6,500 budget to create a garden from scratch that delivers a real 'wow' factor, incorporating dramatic planting, big architectural features and a swanky hot tub area

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including movie posters, Scandinavian furniture and some unusual dolls' heads. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Finland v Belgium (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the final game in Group B for both sides, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium in Russia. Finland, perhaps surprisingly, have an excellent record against the Belgians, and are undefeated against them since 1968, with their two most recent meetings, in 2011 and 2016, both ending in 1-1 draws. They got their campaign under way with a 1-0 victory over Denmark, before losing to Russia by the same scoreline, and will now hope that Roberto Martinez's men will take their foot off the gas, having already secured their place in the next round after winning their opening two matches. Commentary comes from Jonathan Pearce and Martin Keown

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Saskilla examines issues of diversity and inclusion in football over the last few years, talking to Premier League footballers, coaches and managers across the UK, and questioning how much the Football Association has done to fulfil pledges that were made in light of peaceful protests by players in solidarity with the Black Lives Matter movement

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and producer Damian Lewis hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 60th series. Journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events. This new series comes from Hammersmith's Riverside Studios, following the previous run's virtual set-up where the panellists filmed from each of their respective homes throughout lockdown, and is planned to also see the re-introduction of a studio audience

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: RuPaul and judges Michelle Visage and Rhys Nicholson are joined for the grand finale of the series by special guests Olivia Newton-John and her daughter Chloe Lattanzi. By the end of the episode, the first Drag Race winner from Australia or New Zealand will be crowned, and get to add an extra verse to RuPaul's I'm a Winner, Baby

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people to the room, with a lazy Susan, Italian jewellery, a cigar case and a ship's wheel on offer to the five professional dealers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben confronts Whitney about spending time with Callum but she tells him to talk to his husband instead. Rocky's interest in the local houses leaves Sonia suspicious, Bobby and Dana take a big step in their relationship and Lola worries about Isaac's erratic behaviour and obsession with Paul

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Dylan gets cold feet when the time comes to escape Shieldinch, but Cameron's sinister plans continue behind his back. Ash tries to build broken bridges, Rory returns home after his operation and Jac struggles to come to terms with a horrible truth

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Fletch's relationship with Evie grows strained as decisions are made about Andrei's future, and he finds himself forced to break her heart to protect his daughter. Ange and Josh try to avoid each other, but must learn to put personal issues aside when a patient's life hangs in the balance. Overwhelmed with guilt, Jeong confesses his mistake with the drugs cupboard to Dom

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team dives into the case of surf-school owner Jake Peters' murder. The victim was shot in his workshop and Humphrey suspects there is more to the incident than meets the eye when he spots the door was locked from the inside with no other obvious means of entry. Suspicion falls on three pupils who have their own agendas, while grieving wife Katie and best friend Karl Slater are harbouring dark secrets. Away from the workplace, Humphrey continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille and decides to take matters into his own hands when faced with competition for her affections. Caribbean-set detective drama, with guest appearances from Hetti Bywater, Claire Goose and Will Mellor

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Croatia v Scotland. Action from both sides' third and final Group D match, which took place at Hampden Park

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to present a countdown of the best midfielders to play in the European Championships

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Romero's newly stable, blissful life is placed in jeopardy by his latest investigation. Meanwhile, Norman considers holding back certain key facts from Dr Edwards. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman is forced to confront his inner demons as his sessions with Dr Edwards progress. Meanwhile, Norma tries to keep her own problems hidden

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England. A chance to see the final Group D fixture for both sides, which took place at Wembley Stadium. With commentary from Steve Wilson and Danny Murphy

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher presents topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double bill from the Newark antiques fair, with two sets of reds and blues being helped by experts Ben Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of the singles final on day seven of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club. Feliciano Lopez won this title when the tournament was last held in 2019, defeating Gilles Simon in 6-2, 6-7, 7-6. Despite receiving a second-round walkover against the injured Juan Martin del Potro, Lopez very much did things the hard way, with all of his matches going to a deciding set

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a pair of ex-pats who are raring to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Dinosaur
programme.desc: The story of how more than 220 dinosaur bones were found in the Argentinean desert, which were found to have come from a previously undiscovered species, which is the largest land dwelling animal known to have existed. David Attenborough visits the archaeological dig and a laboratory where the remains are being cleaned and analysed with lead scientist Dr Diego Pol and evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod, and meets animators, model-makers, paleontologists and anatomy experts who are working to reconstruct what the 37 metre-long creature would have looked like

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents an archive edition Nature in Art in Gloucestershire, the world's first museum and art gallery dedicated to art inspired by nature. While picking up a few artistic tips, Ellie introduces a selection of stories from the archives that demonstrate how the countryside has been an abiding influence, from early man to modern-day artists braving the extremes to capture a landscape or using wildlife itself to create a picture

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Mark receives word that his father has passed away, and he is given the chance to leave prison for a day to attend the funeral. However, at the last moment, contraband belonging to Daniel is discovered in their cell, and his temporary release is cancelled. He later gets given the chance to take part in a crime and punishment conference, an opportunity Jackson seeks to exploit. Eric is forced to take greater risks to protect his family - but finally has to face the consequences of his actions

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final matches in Group A - Wales v Italy at Stadio Olimpico in Rome and Switzerland v Turkey at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales' triumph against Russia in their concluding group game in 2016 ensured they made it to the knockout stage of a major tournament for the first time since 1958. Today they faced four-time world champions Italy, who have reached at least the quarter-finals of this championship four times out of five this century

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: More highlights from recent series of the motoring show. Featuring another outing for Paddy McGuinness's ice cream van, a tribute to Stirling Moss, the Top Gear Mid Life Crisis Triathlon, and two very different takes on Italian Supercar

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v Wales. A chance to see the final Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. With commentary from Steve Bower and Robbie Savage

programme.title: 'San Fhuil (It's in the Blood)
programme.desc: A personal portrait of a South Uist crofting township where the Greylag geese outnumber people by three to one. Over the last 30 years, the resident population of Greylags has increased from less than 1,000 to over 9,000 birds. With little to check the breeding Greylag, the geese are destroying the cultivation of Uist corn

programme.desc: Betty wants a nice photo of Dave and Anna, but the camera George uses happens to belong to Gran, and it's been cursed by an ancient mummy.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: If there were two things Dave could wish for it would be for Fuzzypeg to smell better, and for Gran to be nicer. Is getting what you want always a good thing?

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: A cute white cat turns up on the Mikkelsen's patio. Everyone thinks it's super-duper adorable, especially Iris.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean chases Scrapper around Mrs Wicket's flat but her sideboard tumbles over, and when Bean sees a bit of fur poking out from under it, he thinks he has squashed Scrapper

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Party Poopers: Ben completely ignores Gwen when she takes him to a family fun center.

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: A brand-new immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Hrvy and Big Zuu join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Woking, Surrey, to transform the garden of John Phillips, a retired serviceman who lost his left arm when a bomb exploded on board HMS Antelope in the Falkland Islands in 1982. The experts plant beds of brightly coloured flowers, a vegetable patch designed to be easily accessible and build a corner seating area, before completing their work with a piece of Falklands rock donated by John's former commanding officer

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Banana, Mouse, Frog, Taco and Kitty perform for guest judge Gabriel Iglesias and the rest of the panell before one of the singers is unmasked and sent home. Nick Cannon presents

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Kitty, Taco, Banana and Frog perform for guest judge T-Pain and the rest of the panel as they are whittled down to three. Nick Cannon presents

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls

programme.title: Junior (1994)
programme.desc: A fertility expert pioneers a treatment enabling men to become pregnant and carry babies to full term. When the authorities cancel funding for the research, he decides to test it on himself, but gives little thought to how difficult it will be to keep it a secret. The situation is further complicated when he starts to fall for the woman whose child he is carrying. Comedy, with Arnold Schwarzenegger, Danny DeVito, Emma Thompson and Frank Langella

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Italy v Wales (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final Group A fixture for both countries, which takes place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. Roberto Mancini's Azzurri became the first side to qualify for the knockout phase on Wednesday evening with their second consecutive 3-0 victory, this time against Switzerland. A draw for Wales will be enough to guarantee their place in the last 16, and victory would ensure they finish top of the group ahead of the Italians. Robert Page will be hoping he can inspire his players to produce a performance reminiscent of October 2002, when Wales famously defeated an Italian side containing the likes of Gianluigi Buffon, Fabio Cannavaro, Andrea Pirlo and Alessandro Del Piero 2-1 in front of 70,000 fans in Cardiff. Page will surely have been calling on the knowledge of midfielder Aaron Ramsey, who plies his trade for Juventus, ahead of this game, while talismanic forward Gareth Bale will also be familiar with the Italian style of play

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Noah makes a discovery. Diane confides in Gabby. Vic drops a bombshell

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: To celebrate 70 years in the film industry, the actress sits down with Piers for a special in which she covers everything from her time as a young starlet appearing alongside Diana Dors and Bette Davis to her reign as Dynasty legend Alexis Carrington. She tells Piers what she really thought of her famous co-stars and ex-husbands, lifts the lid on her tumultuous affair with Warren Beatty and relives the day she discovered her sister Jackie was terminally ill

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: Previously unseen footage from the actress's travelogue programmes, beginning with a look at her journey from Japan to Siberia. The trip begins on the frozen Japanese sea of Okhotsk, and goes across China and Mongolia before arriving at Lake Baikal in Siberia, where Joanna meets some amazing seals

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, Jamie Redknapp and Freddie Flintoff travel around the country to discover each other's family histories. Proud Lancastrian Freddie is anxious that his DNA may lead him to Yorkshire, and there is a surprise in store when the pair arrive the Wakefield Mining Museum. Later, Jamie's family journey takes him to London's East End, where he uncovers a notorious mystery that has remained unsolved for generations

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: North Macedonia v Netherlands (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final Group C fixture for both sides, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. The Macedonians, who are the lowest-ranked team ever to appear in the European Championships, have given a good account of themselves so far, but still lost both matches against Austria and Ukraine, and now face a tough test against the Dutch, who have already booked their place in the next round with two victories. Patrick Vieira, Nigel de Jong and Eni Aluko provide the expert analysis, with commentary from Clive Tyldesley and Ally McCoist

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Alan, Fred, Kathryn and Lucy pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final Group D fixture for both sides, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. These teams are familiar with each other, having been drawn in the same qualifying group for this tournament. The game on this ground was a resounding 5-0 victory for Gareth Southgate's men, with Raheem Sterling claiming a hat-trick. However, the reverse fixture in Prague finished 2-1 to the home side thanks to goals from Jakub Brabec and Zdenek Ondrasek, after Harry Kane had given England an early lead from the penalty spot. That was England's first qualifying defeat since they were beaten by Ukraine in October 2009, and Southgate will be looking for a much better performance this time around. Familiar faces in the Czech squad include West Ham United duo Vladimir Coufal and Tomas Soucek, and Burnley striker Matej Vydra. With analysis from Roy Keane, Gary Neville, Ian Wright, Reshmin Chowdhury, Ashley Cole and Andros Townsend. Co

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: To celebrate 70 years in the film industry, the actress sits down with Piers for a special in which she covers everything from her time as a young starlet appearing alongside Diana Dors and Bette Davis to her reign as Dynasty legend Alexis Carrington. She tells Piers what she really thought of her famous co-stars and ex-husbands, lifts the lid on her tumultuous affair with Warren Beatty and relives the day she discovered her sister Jackie was terminally ill

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Neil Spooner and Tom Dove travel to Thailand, where they have previously caught huge Siamese carp, but this time the focus is on the other species of carp found there. Their adventure is split between Fishing Park Samui and Gillhams Fishing Resorts, where they will have to use all of their skill and knowledge to outwit as many different carp species as possible, and in the process they will be hoping to bank one of the stunning Jullien's golden carp that can be found there

programme.desc: Betty wants a nice photo of Dave and Anna, but the camera George uses happens to belong to Gran, and it's been cursed by an ancient mummy.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: If there were two things Dave could wish for it would be for Fuzzypeg to smell better, and for Gran to be nicer. Is getting what you want always a good thing?

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together

programme.title: LEGO Hidden Side
programme.desc: The mission is simple: collect a package from the train station and bring it to JB. How come Jack and Parker flying through the air and fighting ghosts?

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: It's April Fool's Day. Mike has invited Iris over for a romantic dinner but instead of romance, Iris is more interested in pranking Mike.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: A cute white cat turns up on the Mikkelsen's patio. Everyone thinks it's super-duper adorable, especially Iris.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean chases Scrapper around Mrs Wicket's flat but her sideboard tumbles over, and when Bean sees a bit of fur poking out from under it, he thinks he has squashed Scrapper

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Party Poopers: Ben completely ignores Gwen when she takes him to a family fun center.

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: A brand-new immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Hrvy and Big Zuu join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Woking, Surrey, to transform the garden of John Phillips, a retired serviceman who lost his left arm when a bomb exploded on board HMS Antelope in the Falkland Islands in 1982. The experts plant beds of brightly coloured flowers, a vegetable patch designed to be easily accessible and build a corner seating area, before completing their work with a piece of Falklands rock donated by John's former commanding officer

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Banana, Mouse, Frog, Taco and Kitty perform for guest judge Gabriel Iglesias and the rest of the panell before one of the singers is unmasked and sent home. Nick Cannon presents

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Kitty, Taco, Banana and Frog perform for guest judge T-Pain and the rest of the panel as they are whittled down to three. Nick Cannon presents

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls

programme.title: Junior (1994)
programme.desc: A fertility expert pioneers a treatment enabling men to become pregnant and carry babies to full term. When the authorities cancel funding for the research, he decides to test it on himself, but gives little thought to how difficult it will be to keep it a secret. The situation is further complicated when he starts to fall for the woman whose child he is carrying. Comedy, with Arnold Schwarzenegger, Danny DeVito, Emma Thompson and Frank Langella

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Italy v Wales (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final Group A fixture for both countries, which takes place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. Roberto Mancini's Azzurri became the first side to qualify for the knockout phase on Wednesday evening with their second consecutive 3-0 victory, this time against Switzerland. A draw for Wales will be enough to guarantee their place in the last 16, and victory would ensure they finish top of the group ahead of the Italians. Robert Page will be hoping he can inspire his players to produce a performance reminiscent of October 2002, when Wales famously defeated an Italian side containing the likes of Gianluigi Buffon, Fabio Cannavaro, Andrea Pirlo and Alessandro Del Piero 2-1 in front of 70,000 fans in Cardiff. Page will surely have been calling on the knowledge of midfielder Aaron Ramsey, who plies his trade for Juventus, ahead of this game, while talismanic forward Gareth Bale will also be familiar with the Italian style of play

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Noah makes a discovery. Diane confides in Gabby. Vic drops a bombshell

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: Previously unseen footage from the actress's travelogue programmes, beginning with a look at her journey from Japan to Siberia. The trip begins on the frozen Japanese sea of Okhotsk, and goes across China and Mongolia before arriving at Lake Baikal in Siberia, where Joanna meets some amazing seals

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, Jamie Redknapp and Freddie Flintoff travel around the country to discover each other's family histories. Proud Lancastrian Freddie is anxious that his DNA may lead him to Yorkshire, and there is a surprise in store when the pair arrive the Wakefield Mining Museum. Later, Jamie's family journey takes him to London's East End, where he uncovers a notorious mystery that has remained unsolved for generations

programme.title: Bob's Burgers

Animated comedy, with the voices of H Jon Benjamin and Kristen Schaal

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: A controversial film-maker puts a cow outside the restaurant in a bid to make a statement. Bob is livid, especially when it starts to scare away customers, but he later surprises everyone by treating it as a member of the family

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Tina develops a crush on a martial arts instructor and decides to join his class, but Bob is annoyed when her new hobby interferes with her job at the restaurant. He attempts to straighten things out by visiting the gym, only to suffer a very public humiliation. Animated sitcom, starring H Jon Benjamin

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian spots a chance to further his writing ambitions when his estranged son Dylan, now a teen reality TV star, turns up in Quahog. programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final Group D fixture for both sides, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. These teams are familiar with each other, having been drawn in the same qualifying group for this tournament. The game on this ground was a resounding 5-0 victory for Gareth Southgate's men, with Raheem Sterling claiming a hat-trick. However, the reverse fixture in Prague finished 2-1 to the home side thanks to goals from Jakub Brabec and Zdenek Ondrasek, after Harry Kane had given England an early lead from the penalty spot. That was England's first qualifying defeat since they were beaten by Ukraine in October 2009, and Southgate will be looking for a much better performance this time around. Familiar faces in the Czech squad include West Ham United duo Vladimir Coufal and Tomas Soucek, and Burnley striker Matej Vydra. With analysis from Roy Keane, Gary Neville, Ian Wright, Reshmin Chowdhury, Ashley Cole and Andros Townsend. Co

programme.desc: Rita's shocked to find Sharon in her flat, having taken a beating from Harvey's gang for trying to warn Leanne. Summer is horrified to realise Billy and Todd think she has an eating disorder. Emma flirts with Curtis in the bistro

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three suitors buy an outfit for a singleton, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes they liked best. The first trio try to impress Essex girl Charlotte

programme.title: Secret Crush
programme.desc: Alex hopes to convince perennially single Martin he is the man for him, while self-proclaimed worst dater ever Max is hoping to leave the friendzone with Nicole

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie is caught red-handed. Billy is angry when Jacob confronts him. Aaron is incredulous

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rita's shocked to find Sharon in her flat, having taken a beating from Harvey's gang for trying to warn Leanne. Summer is horrified to realise Billy and Todd think she has an eating disorder. Emma flirts with Curtis in the bistro

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Stephen Boss talks to Garcelle Beauvais about The Real, while Winnie Harlow discusses Making the Cut. Plus, Anson Seabra performs Walking Through Hell

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Michael Jackson impersonator Mitch, bar manager Aiden and student JJ try to impress Becky, a student from Leeds, with their outfit choices

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: The three remaining contestants sing in the final, with all of them being unmasked, but only one can win the Golden Mask Trophy

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Surreal comedy from the mind of Harry Hill, inspired by viewers' clips, featuring the horrors of the Harry Styles Finishing School, the terrifying sight of Beyonce without her make-up, and Sarah Millican fighting for her fame with her trusty nunchaku

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Surreal comedy from the mind of Harry Hill, inspired by viewers' clips, featuring a person resembling Steven Spielberg who finds an explosive cure for his constipation, and a Clare Balding lookalike who marries a horse. Plus, a trampoline electrocution and a drone camera that bumped into a bride on her wedding day

programme.title: Catchphrase: Catchiest Moments
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern presents a compilation of some of his favourite moments from the quiz show, from the funniest answers to the strangest. Featuring previously unseen footage, and giving viewers a chance to play along at home as contestants make unexpected attempts to Say What They See

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final Group D fixture for both sides, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. These teams are familiar with each other, having been drawn in the same qualifying group for this tournament. The game on this ground was a resounding 5-0 victory for Gareth Southgate's men, with Raheem Sterling claiming a hat-trick. However, the reverse fixture in Prague finished 2-1 to the home side thanks to goals from Jakub Brabec and Zdenek Ondrasek, after Harry Kane had given England an early lead from the penalty spot. That was England's first qualifying defeat since they were beaten by Ukraine in October 2009, and Southgate will be looking for a much better performance this time around. Familiar faces in the Czech squad include West Ham United duo Vladimir Coufal and Tomas Soucek, and Burnley striker Matej Vydra. With analysis from Roy Keane, Gary Neville, Ian Wright, Reshmin Chowdhury, Ashley Cole and Andros Townsend. Co

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Mort enlists the help of Peter and Quagmire as he battles overwhelming financial troubles, but things take a turn for the worse when they decide to burn down his pharmacy for the insurance money

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins are stranded near an Amish community when their car breaks down on the way home from an amusement park, and the family launches a crusade against the conservative locals when Meg falls for one of the boys

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Hayley secures an internship with a local news station and starts working with a controversial reporter who harbours an interesting secret. Animated comedy, starring the voice of Rachael MacFarlane

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan tries to infiltrate the Occupy movement, but the mission goes wrong and he is captured by radicals. He is soon returned home, but Hayley suspects that her father may have been brainwashed

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Bob tries to drum up support for the restaurant's grand reopening, but the day takes an unexpected turn when the health inspector pays a visit. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of H Jon Benjamin and Kristen Schaal

programme.title: Hellboy II: The Golden Army (2008)
programme.desc: The demon superhero, aided by a team from the Bureau of Paranormal Research and Defence, leads the fight against renegade elf Prince Nuada who is intent on reawakening an unstoppable robot army and bringing about the demise of mankind. Action fantasy sequel directed by Guillermo del Toro, starring Ron Perlman, Selma Blair, Doug Jones and Luke Goss

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin uses the threat of a roving mouse as an excuse to spend the night in Chrissy's bedroom. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron finds Liv, Bernice is determined and Meena covers her tracks with David

programme.title: Carry On Cowboy (1966)
programme.desc: Notorious outlaw the Rumpo Kid runs riot in lawless Stodge City. A local judge sends for someone to clean up the town - a summons taken literally by a timid English sanitation expert who becomes the new sheriff. Comedy Western, starring Jim Dale, Sid James, Charles Hawtrey, Kenneth Williams, Bernard Bresslaw and Joan Sims

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barrister is killed with a lethal dose of morphine and suspicion falls on ex-convict Grady Felton, who has recently returned to the village where he committed murder years earlier. However, he has a strong alibi and Barnaby thinks he must be working with an accomplice - until Grady himself is targeted in an arson attack. Detective drama, guest starring Sharon Duce, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: As darkness covers Midsomer Stanton during a total eclipse of the sun, Jeremy Harper is killed by a blow to the head with a meteorite. The victim was a leading light in the village - an amateur astronomer who was passionate about preserving Moonstone Ridge, a Bronze Age heritage site housing a 1930s observatory. Barnaby and Jones are called in to investigate, and the duo discover that intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the local stargazing community. Murder mystery, guest starring Maureen Lipman and Kenny Ireland, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The inspector and his sidekick DS Hathaway investigate the murder of a talented maths student. The prime suspect is the victim's ex-boyfriend, who is convinced his uncle has set him up, but old case papers belonging to Morse reveal the boy's family is hiding a dark secret. Laurence Fox and Rebecca Front co-star alongside Kevin Whately

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is called to the University of Northumberland when Jamie Marshall, a promising young journalism student, falls to his death from the top of a disused faculty building. With no eyewitnesses and no immediate leads, Vera turns to friends and family to glean all she can about Jamie, and to discover who might have wanted him dead. The student's innocent facade is revealed to have concealed simmering resentment and anger which he expressed on the internet - and which may have made him some deadly enemies

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A middle-aged Oxford graduate is found murdered, setting in motion an investigation that leads Lewis and Hathaway to one of the university's most senior dons, whose respectable exterior conceals a strange past. Before long a second death occurs, and it becomes apparent that both victims shared a secret - and that someone is out for revenge. Starring Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Anna Massey, Clare Holman and Sian Thomas

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: The duo try to escape their marriage troubles by investing in their future, but a disastrous business venture mires them in debt just as Bobby's playing career starts to wane. Bobby winds up managing a lowly non-league side, and Tina dismisses rumours he's having an affair - until her worst fears are confirmed. When he confesses to loving the woman involved, her world collapses along with her marriage and she finds she must start her life afresh. Drama, starring Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The discovery of a body at a ramshackle farm alerts Wycliffe to the fact that a murderer is at large in a sleepy Cornish village - and when two locals disappear, he realises it will take all his deductive powers to solve the mystery. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Having spent the night in the ginnel, Nina staggers home, lying to Roy about where she has been. Aled's consultant confirms he is an ideal candidate for a cochlear implant operation, while a troubled Mary tells Bernie what she heard Dev saying to Steve. When Evelyn calls to pick up the girls, she's gobsmacked by Tyrone's new look, and Billy gets a call from Summer's school

programme.desc: Aaron finds Liv, Bernice is determined and Meena covers her tracks with David

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ida threatens Mike with industrial action if he does not reinstate Hayley. Hayley is amazed that people are prepared to strike over her, and Greg has some home truths for Maxine

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine tells Sally she has finished with Greg. Curly tells Toyah he was hurt by her article, and Kevin tells Sally he cannot cope with her hostility

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Roy's dinner party is a disaster and the owner of the cottage phones the police, while Liam enjoys a drink with Zoe

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe grows more suspicious of Liam, Roy prepares for court, and Richie turns down Tricia's offer of a date

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick and Jo buy an engagement ring, but receive a hostile response to their news from her mother. Greengrass angers Blaketon by standing bail for a burglar. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry, Juliette Gruber and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid resolves to exercise a little discipline when Sally adopts a stray dog. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin borrow Mr Roper's car to drive to Southampton for a football match. However, the vehicle has similar qualities to its unreliable owner and soon breaks down leaving the duo having to summon help from an unexpected quarter. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A special constable mysteriously disappears after a row with pig farmer Betty Sutch. His uniform is discovered in the pig sty and macabre rumours begin to circulate the village that implicate his wife. Meanwhile, Greengrass benefits from a cleaner house when he puts up an old friend. Nick Berry and Bill Maynard star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe keeps her suspicions about the kidnapper to herself. Roy finds prison life hard to cope with. Kathy tells Ashley she does not want to talk about Alice

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe and Terry get a shock when they go to search Liam's house. Kelly pours her heart out to her stepbrother. Nixon threatens Roy in prison

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine tells Kevin she saw Sally in Greg's flat. Alma refuses to forgive Mike for sacking Hayley. Eric has some troubling news for Curly about Firman's

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ida and the girls celebrate when Hayley returns to work. Rita is annoyed when Alec proposes to her again. Greg tells Sally to leave Kevin

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A ritual killing in a village church points to the work of a satanic cult, but the detective thinks local animosity might actually hold the key to the mystery. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Sybil's determination to pursue her political leanings lands her in great danger when a fight breaks out at an election results meeting. Matthew's tenderness in caring for the would-be suffragette is noticed by her sisters, but it is Mary who is stunned to receive a marriage proposal. Bates is cleared of suspicion over the stolen wine, but offers his resignation rather than name the culprit. Drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective suspects a connection between the death of a student with secret plans for advancement and the killing of well-known criminal Nicky Turnball, who was in Oxford to speak at the Union. As Lewis and Hathaway eliminate suspects from a long list of the crook's enemies, the investigation brings him into contact with old flame Diane - who also happens to be Turnball's wife. Kevin Whately stars, with Laurence Fox and Gina McKee

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: The queen is moved by the plight of a silk weaver in Spitalfields, and decides to throw a lavish medieval ball at the palace to help, with all attendees wearing outfits made in the area. Albert has his doubts about the plan, but finds himself seduced by the grandeur of the event. However, while the evening proves a success, reuniting old friends and creating new romances, it is greeted with outrage by the discontented public. Victoria worries over the lack of news about Lord Melbourne, and sends Emma to track down her old friend

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: The royal court celebrates the birth of the Queen's second child, but Victoria alone is unhappy as she struggles to bond with her newborn son. A crisis compels Albert to return to Coburg, separating the couple for the first time since their wedding, and on his arrival, he learns a terrible family secret that leaves him plunged into torment. Skerrett makes a disastrous mistake that puts Francatelli in jeopardy, while Drummond's relationship with Lord Alfred comes under threat by a reluctant revelation

programme.desc: Ida is confident she can settle a mix-up over Robert's birthday
